evals.name: bootstrap-creates-correct-state
evals.prompt: You are running /web-evolve landing for the first time on a project. The project has src/components/landing/HeroSection.tsx (120 lines) and src/components/landing/PricingTable.tsx (95 lines). No .web-evolve/ directory exists. Describe exactly what state.json you would write after Step 1A bootstrap. Include all fields.
evals.rationale: Tests that Step 1A bootstrap writes all required v2 fields at correct initial values.

evals.name: scope-switch-adds-new-areas
evals.prompt: state.json has last_scope: landing and two landing areas both status: done. User runs /web-evolve /clients. What happens in Step 1 and what does state.json look like after?
expected_assertions: Step 1 bootstraps /clients areas without removing landing areas
evals.rationale: Tests scope-switch mid-project (Present branch, no areas for new scope).

evals.name: needs-work-pass-reset
evals.prompt: An area has status: needs-work, pass: 2, score: 67. Fresh session starts, Step 2 picks this area. When Step 5 begins, what is area.pass and why?
expected_assertions: area.pass reset to 0 at start of Step 5
reason references prior session pass being stale
evals.rationale: Tests needs-work re-entry resets pass to 0. Without this, Step 5 immediately exits with zero fix attempts.

evals.name: context-budget-halt-after-scoring
evals.prompt: Skill('clarify') was called earlier this session (session_skill_calls=1). In Step 5, need to call Skill('visual-uplift'). Area scored: Hook:18, Visuals:11, Clarity:21, Function:20 (total 70). What happens and what does state.json look like?
expected_assertions: state: status=needs-work, score=70, dimensions written, pass written
evals.rationale: Tests context budget halt after scoring — state must be persisted for next session resume.
